summary_lines: ADF now treats human-readable command flags as the default for operator-facing playbooks.
The ASMS UI host-pressure step now uses `uptime && free -h && df -h /`.
The Starlight generator now preserves `node_modules` during regeneration so remote review refreshes do not unnecessarily break the page.

handoff_summary: Keep applying human-readable flags like `-h` and `--no-pager` in operator-facing playbooks.
Use the live review page at `/playbooks/asms-ui-is-down/` to verify future wording changes.
The next content work still stays under `deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks`.
highest_risk_observation: The remote review URL currently depends on an active Astro process on `adf-dev`; the generator-side `node_modules` preservation fix reduces future breakage, but the review surface still needs a healthy remote dev server.

memory_tier.summary: Restart memory after adopting the human-readable command standard for operator-facing playbooks.

memory_validity.summary: High-confidence restart memory because the wording contract, generated playbook, and live review page were all checked.

summary_lines: Do not use `free -m` as the default operator-facing example when `free -h` is enough for the decision.
Do not remove generated Starlight `node_modules` during routine site regeneration.

recommended_next_action: Recommended next action: keep applying the readability standard as additional real diagnostic playbooks are published under the canonical template.
resolved_block_summary: The readability rule is now explicit in the language standard, visible on the live ASMS UI page, and protected by the Starlight regeneration fix.
summary: ADF now defaults to human-readable operator commands and the live ASMS UI review page reflects that standard.
